name: effective-weight-endpoint-content-v1
frozen_at: 2026-07-17 before any scientific effective-weight patch outcome; singular spectra and an algebra-only complete-delta endpoint identity had already been inspected, and the identity tolerance was prospectively corrected before the first scientific cell after two plumbing-only MPS checks measured valid-token relative L2 error below 4.75e-7
question: Does the matched-lineage ds2 preference-minus-control LoRA endpoint difference contain a compact effective-weight subspace that bidirectionally carries both held-out wolf behavior and held-out preference-number fit, a broader shared late write port, or no stable endpoint content despite the previously established tangent overlap?

scope.responsibility: A tested effective-weight intervention is locally responsible only when the same frozen patch both moves held-out wolf behavior and improves held-out fit to the preference-teacher numeric distribution in the preferenceward direction, with reciprocal effects when removed from the preference endpoint.
scope.effective_weight: For target module m, DeltaW_m=(alpha_lora/r)[B_preference A_preference-B_control A_control]=2[B_preference A_preference-B_control A_control]. Raw LoRA A/B coordinates are never compared or patched.
scope.local_reversibility_limit: The strongest permitted positive claim is a local dual-use reversible effective-weight subspace. It is not global neural-network invertibility, a unique inverse, or proof that wolf preference is necessary for numerical fit.
scope.identity_limit: Adding the complete all-module endpoint difference necessarily maps one saved LoRA endpoint to the other up to floating-point ordering error. That is an implementation guard only and is excluded from scientific classification.
scope.endpoint_limit: Failure of the endpoint-content assay does not by itself prove transient overlap. It leaves the previously measured transient-tangent account viable; temporal patching would be required to establish transience causally.

early_control.energy_match: multiply every early singular value by late_group_frobenius_norm/early_group_frobenius_norm separately within each seed

svd.method: thin QR of the two rank-8 factors followed by SVD of the at-most-16x16 core
svd.degenerate_component_policy: Primary inference uses cumulative prefixes only; no individual singular-vector semantic claim is permitted.

sham.construction: independent CPU float64 Gaussian QR bases in each module input/output space with the exact real singular values and prefix energy

endpoint_semantics.control: restore control and add +a times the frozen intervention
endpoint_semantics.preference: restore preference and add -a times the frozen intervention

bootstrap.interval: paired percentile 95%; resample six frozen prompt clusters for behavior and eight frozen paired numeric-row blocks for both numerical outcomes

gates.replicated_bidirectional_joint_effect: For every seed separately and both endpoint directions, all three benefit 95% lower bounds exceed zero.
gates.spectrum_sham_specificity: For the same rank and alpha=1, every seed and endpoint has positive 95% lower bounds for all three paired real-minus-sham benefit contrasts.
gates.local_slope: At alpha=0.25, all three benefit point estimates are positive in every seed and endpoint; this is a direction check, not a significance gate.
gates.coefficient_sign_consistency: At alpha in {0.25,0.5,0.75,1.0}, all three benefit point estimates remain positive in every seed and endpoint; non-monotone magnitudes are reported but are not forbidden because the network is nonlinear.
gates.local_dual_use_reversible_subspace_suppo: At least one predeclared per-module rank k in {1,2,4,8} has at least a 1% relative singular-value gap at every selected module boundary in both seeds, passes joint alpha=1, coefficient sign consistency (including the alpha=0.25 local slope), and same-k sham specificity. Report the smallest passing k.
gates.shared_late_write_port_supported: No compact prefix passes, but the full late per-module rank-16 intervention passes the replicated bidirectional joint-effect and rank-16 sham-specificity gates.
gates.endpoint_content_not_supported_tangent_o: The full late rank-16 intervention fails the replicated bidirectional joint-effect gate while both complete-delta identity guards pass. This does not itself prove transience.
gates.mixed_non_specific: Use when an effect exists but fails the predeclared specificity or classification gates.
